title: WP-KERNEL-009 Editor (Tiptap+Monaco) and Loom (Heaper LoomBlock library surface) full-feature capability inventory
purpose: Spec-accurate capability inventory for two Handshake-native surfaces: (A) the rich editor as a genuine VS Code-class editing replacement (Tiptap prose + embedded Monaco), and (B) Loom as a Heaper-derived LoomBlock library+context surface (NOT an Obsidian clone). Each capability is mapped to a Handshake-native implementation target, a covering WP-009 MT, and a BUILT/PARTIAL/MISSING status from a read-only audit of the feat/WP-KERNEL-009 worktree.

audit_basis.governance_worktree: D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake Worktrees/wt-gov-kernel (.GOV authority + skeleton only)
audit_basis.note: The gov_kernel worktree the prompt pointed at contains only .GOV governance + an app/src skeleton (components/diagnostics). The real WP-009 product code lives in the sibling wtc-kernel-009 worktree; all BUILT/PARTIAL/MISSING verdicts below were read from there.
key_files_read: migrations 0013_loom_mvp, 0130-0142 knowledge_*, 0139 wiki_projections, 0140 rich_documents, 0170/0291 code_files, 0280-0282 document_identity/embeds/backlinks

spec_authority: Master Spec v02.192 module 11 §10.12 'Loom (Heaper-style Library Surface)' — full Loom integration spec (LoomBlock, LoomEdge, 4 views, cache tiers, dedup, AI jobs, schemas, FR events, conformance, Heaper feature matrix Appendix A)

§10.12 #9.1.1 'WP-KERNEL-009 authority supersession' — for WP-009, PostgreSQL/EventLedger is the ONLY authority path; SQLite refs in the imported Loom section are legacy context, not authority/cache/offline/bootstrap/proof
§2.2.1.14 LoomBlock Entity; §2.3.7.1 Loom Relational Edges (LoomEdge); §2.3.13.7 Loom Storage Trait
§2.3.13.11 RichDocument authority record (versioned ProseMirror/Tiptap JSON + schema_version + CRDT refs + EventLedger promotion + projection refs); §7.1.1.8 editor authority + feature surface
MT-145..MT-160 RichDocumentCore (canonical doc model); MT-161..MT-176 TiptapMonacoIntegration (editor); MT-177..MT-192 LoomObsidianNavigation (Loom)
MT-187 ObsidianImportBoundary — markdown vault MUST NOT be authority; Loom may IMPORT from markdown-like notes only

framing_confirmation.loom_is_not_obsidian: Loom is framed as the Heaper-derived LoomBlock library+context surface. Its atom is the LoomBlock (block-as-unit-of-meaning): note, file, annotated_file (file fused with rich-text context as ONE unit), tag_hub (a tag that is itself a block with content + sub-tags), or journal. Obsidian/Logseq/Roam/Tana are cited as borrowable UX idioms only (link/tag/backlink/graph/quick-switch/unlinked-mention/block-ref/supertag), never clone targets.
framing_confirmation.markdown_vault_never_authority: Respected throughout. Per §9.1.1 + MT-187 + [LM-AUTH], LoomBlock/LoomEdge/views/search/backlinks/graph/CRDT-history/AI-proposals MUST resolve to PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed authority. A generated wiki page, markdown vault, browser view state, or imported Heaper-style file layout MUST NOT become authority. Markdown is an IMPORT source and an export PROJECTION only.
framing_confirmation.heaper_source_pattern: Heaper is the source pattern (4 principles: local-first, CRDT/Yjs sync, relational organization 'links are the new folders', ownership; 9 UX patterns H-1..H-9). Loom EXTENDS the existing unified-node schema / Knowledge Graph / Raw-Derived-Display / CRDT / Storage-Portability — it introduces no parallel infrastructure.
framing_confirmation.handshake_native_runtime: All targets use bundled/lockfile-governed Tiptap 3.x + Monaco + Yjs (and CodeMirror only if ever needed) over the existing knowledge/loom/code-index/retrieval backend on PostgreSQL/EventLedger. NO external VS Code/Obsidian process, NO CDN, NO Docker-only path, NO SQLite-as-authority for WP-009, NO markdown vault as authority.

status_legend.BUILT: Implementation present and exercised by tests/fixtures in the wtc-kernel-009 worktree (backend storage/API or frontend lib module).
status_legend.PARTIAL: Core primitive or backend exists but the full VS Code-class / spec-complete behavior or the assembled UI surface is incomplete.
status_legend.MISSING: No meaningful implementation found for this capability in the audited worktree.
status_legend.mt_lifecycle_note: MT lifecycle status in the contracts: RichDocumentCore (145-160) = READY_FOR_VALIDATION; TiptapMonacoIntegration (161-176) and LoomObsidianNavigation (177-192) = PENDING. NOTE: many 161-176 editor primitives already exist as tested lib modules even though the MT is PENDING (the MT tracks the assembled+validated editor, not just the primitive). Status columns reflect CODE PRESENT, not MT lifecycle.

editor_vscode_class.name: Rich prose document core (paragraph/heading/lists/quote/marks)
editor_vscode_class.description: StarterKit-backed prose: paragraphs, h1-h3 headings, bullet/ordered lists, blockquote, bold/italic/inline-code/link marks.
editor_vscode_class.handshake_native_target: Bundled @tiptap/starter-kit 3.x in buildWp009ExtensionSet; serialized to RichDocument block-tree (18 kinds).
editor_vscode_class.notes: extension_set.ts + extension_inventory.ts + block_tree.rs BlockKind (Paragraph..SymbolLink). Tested.

editor_vscode_class.name: Tables
editor_vscode_class.description: Insertable tables with header row, rows, cells.
editor_vscode_class.handshake_native_target: @tiptap/extension-table TableKit (resizable off); backend block kind 'table'.
editor_vscode_class.notes: TableKit wired; table.insert command (3x3 + header). Graceful-degrade extension.

editor_vscode_class.name: Task lists / checkboxes
editor_vscode_class.description: Nested task lists with checkboxes (todo items).
editor_vscode_class.handshake_native_target: @tiptap/extension-list TaskList/TaskItem; backend kinds task_list/task_item.
editor_vscode_class.notes: list.task command present.

editor_vscode_class.name: Schema versioning + migration round-trip
editor_vscode_class.description: Persisted ProseMirror/Tiptap JSON carries a schema version; load asserts/migrates on mismatch.
editor_vscode_class.handshake_native_target: WP009_RICH_DOCUMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION stamped on RichDocument.schema_version; schema_versioning.ts migrations; knowledge_rich_document_versions table.
editor_vscode_class.notes: schema_versioning.ts + versions migration (0140). Migration ladder is v1-only so far.

editor_vscode_class.name: Embedded Monaco code block (in-document code editor)
editor_vscode_class.description: Code-block node whose NodeView mounts a REAL Monaco editor: language-aware, writes code+language+round-trip-hash to node attrs; graceful <textarea> fallback if Monaco fails.
editor_vscode_class.handshake_native_target: MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx + monaco_code_block_node.ts + monaco/setup.ts (bundled workers, offline, no CDN). Persisted via knowledge_editor_code_nodes.
editor_vscode_class.notes: Real Monaco mount with degrade path + stable data-testids. THIS IS A CODE-BLOCK EMBED, NOT A FULL WORKBENCH.

editor_vscode_class.name: Monaco language registration + detection
editor_vscode_class.description: Curated language id registry + detection for code blocks; theme/diagnostics/formatting hooks scaffold.
editor_vscode_class.handshake_native_target: monaco/language_registry.ts; setModelLanguage on picker change.
editor_vscode_class.notes: Registry + language picker BUILT. Per-language diagnostics/formatting hooks are scaffolded but not fully wired to backend LSP/code-index.

editor_vscode_class.name: Monaco worker bundling (offline, no CDN)
editor_vscode_class.description: Monaco web workers bundled under Handshake packaging; offline/local startup proven; no CDN fetch.
editor_vscode_class.handshake_native_target: monaco/worker_bundling.ts + worker_map.ts under Tauri/vite bundling.
editor_vscode_class.notes: worker_map + worker_bundling with tests.

editor_vscode_class.name: Auto code-block rules (fenced/indented paste, language hints, prose<->code)
editor_vscode_class.description: Fenced paste, indented paste, language hints, slash-command code blocks, reversible prose/code conversion.
editor_vscode_class.handshake_native_target: tiptap/auto_code_block_rules.ts + editor/auto_code_block.ts + code_block_serialization.ts.
editor_vscode_class.notes: Tested input rules + serialization.

loom.name: LoomBlock entity (block-as-unit-of-meaning)
loom.description: First-class workspace entity with global UUID; content_type one of note/file/annotated_file/tag_hub/journal; references a Document and/or Asset without duplicating them; title independent of filename.
loom.handshake_native_target: storage/loom.rs LoomBlock + NewLoomBlock; create/get/update/delete via storage trait (postgres+sqlite); api/loom.rs block routes; for WP-009 must resolve to ProjectKnowledgeIndex/EventLedger authority (MT-177).
loom.notes: Loom-MVP LoomBlock fully BUILT (model + CRUD + dedup + derived metrics) on loom_blocks table. MT-177 LoomBlockKnowledgeBridge (link LoomBlock to ProjectKnowledge entities/rich-docs/assets/spans, PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority) is PENDING — this is the WP-009 bridge layer.

loom.name: annotated_file (file + rich-text context fused as ONE unit)
loom.spec_anchor: §10.12 Pattern H-2 / [LM-BLOCK-003] / Appendix A 'File + rich text context: ADOPT'
loom.description: A file and its rich-text context are one block (context is part of block identity, not a sidecar). Both are RawContent; neither silently AI-modified.
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomBlock with both document_id (Document context) + asset_id (Asset file); content_type=annotated_file.
loom.notes: Content type + dual-ref model BUILT in loom.rs. The fused editing UX (open file, edit its story inline, search-by-context) is not assembled; bridge to rich-doc context is MT-177.

loom.name: tag_hub (tags are themselves blocks with content + sub-tags)
loom.description: A #tag is a first-class LoomBlock (content_type=tag_hub) carrying its own rich-text content, sub-tags (SUB_TAG edges), and backlinks. 'Even your tags are just notes.' Closest external analog: Tana supertags.
loom.handshake_native_target: TagHub LoomBlock + SUB_TAG edges; api/loom MT-182 implements tags-as-blocks with backlinks, nested tags, search filters.
loom.notes: TagHub content type + SubTag edge type defined in loom.rs. MT-182 TagsAndTagHubs (full tag-hub surface) PENDING.

loom.name: journal / daily notes
loom.description: Date-keyed LoomBlock (content_type=journal, journal_date). Logseq/Roam daily-notes idiom.
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomBlock journal_date field (present in loom.rs + 0013 migration).
loom.notes: journal_date field + Journal content type BUILT in model; no daily-notes UI/flow and no WP-009 MT explicitly owns journal UX.

loom.name: @mention edges (referential, 'has a relationship with')
loom.description: @mention creates a MENTION edge (RawContent, user-authored), stable across renames (by UUID), inline-anchored with source offsets; auto-creates a NOTE block if target missing (Obsidian wikilink idiom).
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomEdge edge_type=Mention + LoomSourceAnchor; api/loom edge routes; for WP-009 persists as knowledge_edges with stable relationship id (backlink.rs).
loom.notes: Edge model + source-anchor + create/delete BUILT in loom.rs. Auto-create-on-missing-target and rename stability proofs are MT-178; inline editor extraction is BUILT in backlink.rs.

loom.name: #tag edges (categorical, 'is a kind of')
loom.description: #tag creates a TAG edge (RawContent). AI-suggested tags are AI_SUGGESTED edges (DerivedContent) until user-confirmed, then become TAG (RawContent).
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomEdge edge_type=Tag / AiSuggested; user-accept promotes to Tag.
loom.notes: Tag + AiSuggested edge types BUILT. AI-suggested->confirmed promotion flow not assembled; full surface is MT-182.

loom.name: Sub-tag hierarchy
loom.description: Nested tags via SUB_TAG edges (e.g. #project/alpha -> SUB_TAG(alpha, project)). Obsidian nested-tag idiom.
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomEdge edge_type=SubTag traversal in tag-hub queries.
loom.notes: SubTag edge type defined; nested-tag resolution + UI is MT-182 (PENDING).

loom.name: Inline source anchors (offset-precise edges)
loom.description: Mentions/tags carry document_id + block_id + char offset_start/end so backlinks can show the exact in-text context.
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomSourceAnchor on LoomEdge; backlink.rs ties to stable block ids (MT-148).
loom.notes: Anchor model BUILT. Wiring editor offsets -> anchored edges -> context-snippet backlinks is MT-178.

loom.name: Backlinks (linked mentions, with context snippet)
loom.description: Every block surface shows all blocks referencing it via MENTION/TAG edges, with a surrounding-text context snippet; reactive on edge create/delete.
loom.handshake_native_target: storage get_backlinks (postgres+sqlite) + knowledge_document_backlinks table (0282); api/loom; backlink panel in DocumentView.
loom.notes: BACKEND BUILT: get_backlinks trait impl + backlink extraction + backlinks migration. Context-snippet + reactive backlinks PANEL UI not assembled; MT-178 BacklinkComputation PENDING.

loom.name: Unlinked mentions (triage)
loom.description: Text occurrences of a block's title/aliases that are NOT yet formal links, surfaced so one click converts them to edges. Obsidian unlinked-mentions idiom; also feeds the Unlinked VIEW.
loom.handshake_native_target: Scan index for title/alias occurrences without an edge; MT-178 computes unlinked mentions with evidence.
loom.notes: No unlinked-mention scanner yet. (The Unlinked VIEW filter exists; 'unlinked MENTIONS' = potential-but-unmade links is a distinct feature.) MT-178 names it.

loom.name: Local graph (neighborhood)
loom.description: Graph of a block's immediate neighborhood with filters, edge-type selection, depth, stale markers, source citations.
loom.handshake_native_target: storage traverse_graph (recursive CTE, postgres+sqlite) + api/loom /graph/traverse (depth default 3, max 8); MT-179 adds filters/stale/citations.
loom.notes: BACKEND graph traversal BUILT (traverse_graph + /loom/graph/traverse). Filtered local-graph API w/ stale markers + a rendered graph view is MT-179 (PENDING).

loom.name: Global graph (project-level)
loom.description: Whole-project graph with performance limits, hub suppression, force/group/color controls. Obsidian global-graph idiom.
loom.handshake_native_target: Project-scoped traversal with limits + hub suppression; rendered force graph.
loom.notes: No global-graph API or view. MT-180 GlobalGraphApi PENDING.

loom.name: Loom view: All (chronological feed)
loom.description: All LoomBlocks sorted by updated_at DESC; infinite-scroll grid or list (photo-stream feel).
loom.handshake_native_target: storage query_all_view + api/loom /views/all; grid/list React surface.
loom.notes: BACKEND BUILT (query_all_view + /views/:view_type). The four-views workspace UI is not assembled in the app shell; MT-183 owns pins/unlinked/sorted surface.

loom.name: Loom view: Unlinked (triage queue)
loom.description: Blocks with zero MENTION+TAG edges (backlink+mention+tag count == 0); items disappear as you link them ('sorting feels like progress'); MUST update in real time.
loom.handshake_native_target: storage query_unlinked_view (join loom_block_metrics) + api/loom; reactive UI.
loom.notes: BACKEND BUILT (query_unlinked_view + metrics). Real-time-disappear UI not assembled.

loom.name: Loom view: Sorted (grouped by tag/mention)
loom.description: Blocks grouped by their TAG/MENTION targets; expandable group headers, each group a mini-feed.
loom.handshake_native_target: storage query_sorted_view -> LoomViewGroup; api/loom; grouped UI.
loom.notes: BACKEND BUILT (query_sorted_view + LoomViewGroup). Grouped UI not assembled.

loom.name: Loom view: Pins (favorites quick-access)
loom.description: Pinned blocks; user-reorderable grid. (Obsidian 'bookmarks/starred' analog.)
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomBlock.pinned + query_pinned_view + api/loom; reorderable grid.
loom.notes: BACKEND BUILT (pinned flag + query_pinned_view). Pins+favorites UI + reorder is MT-183.

loom.name: View filters (content_type/MIME/date/tag/mention) + grid/list toggle
loom.description: Filter views by content type, file MIME, date range, specific tags/mentions; switch grid (media) vs list (notes) layout.
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomViewFilters in loom.rs + api/loom query params; layout toggle in UI.
loom.notes: Filter struct BUILT; grid/list toggle is UI-level, not assembled.

loom.name: Folder tree + color labels + sort modes
loom.description: Persistent folder hierarchy, labels, colors, sort modes, project membership. ('Links are the new folders' but an explicit tree is still offered; Obsidian file-tree idiom.)
loom.handshake_native_target: PostgreSQL-persisted folder hierarchy/labels/colors/sort (MT-181); folder tree panel reusing WorkspaceSidebar shell.
loom.notes: WorkspaceSidebar today lists workspaces/documents/canvases only — NO Loom folder tree, colors, labels, or sort modes. MT-181 FolderTreeAndColorLabels PENDING.

loom.name: File import pipeline + checksum dedup (drag duplicate -> navigate)
loom.description: Import computes SHA-256, checks workspace for duplicate; on hit, navigate to the existing LoomBlock instead of copying; else store Asset + create LoomBlock + queue preview + emit FR event.
loom.handshake_native_target: api/loom /loom/import (sha2 hashing + find_by_content_hash dedup) + loom_fs.rs blob storage; FR-EVT-LOOM-006 dedup_hit.
loom.notes: Import + dedup + asset blob storage BUILT in api/loom.rs + loom_fs.rs (loom-MVP). Workspace-scoped dedup index present (0013).

loom.name: Cache-tiered media browsing (preview pyramid, on-demand full-res)
loom.description: Tier0 metadata / Tier1 preview (thumbnail/waveform/poster/first-page) / Tier2 proxy / Tier3 original; previews replicated, originals on-demand; video streams without full local cache.
loom.handshake_native_target: Asset proxy/preview fields (is_proxy_of/proxy_asset_id) + thumbnail endpoint; preview-tier config persisted per device.
loom.notes: Asset proxy/thumbnail fields + /assets/:id/thumbnail endpoint BUILT; full 4-tier cache config + streaming + per-device replication NOT implemented and NO WP-009 MT owns the cache-tier system (Loom-roadmap LM-P1-005/LM-P2-006).

loom.name: Thumbnail/preview generation job
loom.description: Mechanical job generating image thumbnails, video poster frames, audio waveforms, document first-page renders; must not block import; FR-logged.
loom.handshake_native_target: Mechanical Tool Bus job producing DerivedContent previews; PreviewStatus on LoomBlockDerived.
loom.notes: PreviewStatus enum exists in model; no generation job. NO WP-009 MT (Loom-roadmap LM-P1-005).

loom.name: Loom search (titles/text/tags/source kinds/symbols/WP/MT/manual)
loom.description: Search across block titles, body text, tags, source kinds, code symbols, WPs, MTs, UserManual pages; filterable by graph relationships. (Obsidian search-operators idiom.)
loom.handshake_native_target: storage search_loom_blocks + api/loom /loom/search + knowledge_retrieval; for WP-009 direct id load precedes hybrid retrieval (§9.1.1).
loom.notes: Loom search backend BUILT (search_loom_blocks). Cross-source search over knowledge index + symbols + WP/MT/manual (MT-186 GraphSearchAndFilters) PENDING. Knowledge retrieval w/ RetrievalTrace is BUILT separately.

loom.name: Quick switcher (jump to any block/note by title)
loom.description: Fuzzy 'open anything by name' switcher (Obsidian Cmd-O / Roam Cmd-O idiom).
loom.handshake_native_target: Title/alias index -> quick-switch palette over LoomBlocks + rich docs.
loom.notes: No quick switcher. Borrowable idiom not yet specced into an MT (search MT-186 is operator-search, not the fuzzy switcher). GAP.

loom.name: Wiki/topic-page projection compiler (citations, stale markers, regenerate)
loom.description: Generate wiki/topic pages from indexed sources with citations, stale markers, regeneration controls. These are DisplayContent PROJECTIONS, never authority.
loom.handshake_native_target: knowledge_wiki_projections table (0139) + projection compiler; render as read view with regenerate.
loom.notes: Wiki projection table + WikiLoom renderer BUILT; full compiler w/ citations+stale-markers+regenerate controls (MT-184) PENDING.

loom.name: Editable wiki overlay (annotate without making projection authority)
loom.description: Operator annotations / editable overlay on a generated wiki page WITHOUT the generated projection text becoming canonical authority.
loom.handshake_native_target: Overlay annotations stored as their own authority rows; projection stays regenerable/non-authority.
loom.notes: No editable-overlay mechanism. MT-185 WikiPageEditableOverlay PENDING. Directly enforces the markdown/projection-never-authority rule.

loom.name: Markdown-like import boundary (vault NEVER authority)
loom.description: Import behavior from markdown-like notes IF needed, while rejecting any markdown vault / imported file layout as authority. Loom may borrow Obsidian's note shape on import; it never adopts the vault as the source of truth.
loom.handshake_native_target: knowledge_document/import.rs converts to RichDocument/LoomBlock authority rows in PostgreSQL/EventLedger; importer emits warnings; no vault canonicalization.
loom.notes: Import path BUILT (import.rs) and aligned with the boundary; the explicit MT-187 'reject vault as authority' guard/proof is PENDING. THIS IS THE KEY ANTI-OBSIDIAN-CLONE BOUNDARY.

loom.name: Navigation breadcrumbs (project/folder/doc/block/symbol/WP/MT/span)
loom.description: Breadcrumb trail across the full entity spine so any block is locatable. (Borrows VS Code/Obsidian breadcrumb idiom but over Handshake entities.)
loom.handshake_native_target: Breadcrumb API over knowledge graph + folder tree; rendered above doc/loom surfaces.
loom.notes: No breadcrumb API/UI. MT-188 NavigationBreadcrumbs PENDING. Shared with editor ED-NAV-005.

loom.name: Loom retrieval-bias visibility (tags/backlinks/pins influence retrieval)
loom.description: Show WHEN Loom tags/backlinks/pins/unlinked-state bias QueryPlan filters / graph expansion / reranking, and expose the graph-bias reasons. Exact id requests resolve by direct load before semantic search.
loom.handshake_native_target: RetrievalTrace decisions surface graph-bias reasons; api/knowledge_retrieval exposes QueryPlan+RetrievalTrace.
loom.notes: RetrievalTrace + QueryPlan + replay BUILT in knowledge_retrieval (decisions JSONB, trace recorded). Surfacing Loom-graph-bias REASONS specifically + a visibility UI (MT-189) PENDING.

loom.name: Loom EventLedger / Flight Recorder events
loom.description: Emit events for view open, search, link create/delete, tag, pin, projection rebuild, repair, dedup hit, preview generated.
loom.handshake_native_target: FlightRecorder events from api/loom + EventLedger; FR-EVT-LOOM-001..012.
loom.notes: api/loom imports FlightRecorder and emits some events; full FR-EVT-LOOM-001..012 coverage + EventLedger binding (MT-190) PENDING.

loom.name: Loom visual-debug views (graph/backlink/folder/search snapshots)
loom.description: Expose graph/backlink/folder/search state through visual-debug snapshots + backend debug payloads for no-context models.
loom.handshake_native_target: Stable selectors + debug-state endpoints mirroring editor visual_debug.ts.
loom.notes: No Loom visual-debug surface yet (the editor has visual_debug.ts; Loom side does not). MT-191 PENDING.

loom.name: CRDT-tracked links/tags (Yjs site ids, conflict-free)
loom.description: Block creation, linking, tagging propagate via Yjs CRDT with site ids; concurrent tagging merges (set semantics); AI suggestions use the AI site id.
loom.handshake_native_target: LoomEdge.crdt_site_id + knowledge_crdt subsystem; AI uses its own site id.
loom.notes: crdt_site_id field on edges + knowledge_crdt graph-proposal subsystem BUILT (0150-0155); full Loom-edge CRDT propagation loop is partial / future (Loom-roadmap Phase 3).

loom.name: AI auto-tag / auto-caption / batch-link suggestions
loom.description: loom_auto_tag (local small), loom_auto_caption (vision), loom_batch_link (suggest groupings for unlinked blocks); all produce AI_SUGGESTED DerivedContent until user-confirmed.
loom.handshake_native_target: AI Job Model profiles writing AiSuggested edges + LoomBlockDerived.auto_tags/auto_caption.
loom.notes: Derived fields (auto_tags/auto_caption/quality_score) + AiSuggested edge exist in model; a 0046 ai_tag_suggestions migration exists; no WP-009 MT owns the AI Loom jobs (Loom-roadmap Phase 2).

loom.name: Loom navigation fixtures (backlinks/unlinked/tags/colors/graph/stale-wiki/exact-open)
loom.description: Fixtures for backlinks, unlinked mentions, tags, folder color labels, graph filters, stale wiki, and exact LoomBlock opens.
loom.handshake_native_target: storage_conformance.rs + frontend fixtures covering each Loom navigation path.
loom.notes: MT-192 LoomNavigationFixtures PENDING; depends on 178-191 being implemented.

loom.name: Canvas integration (drag LoomBlock onto canvas, no copy)
loom.description: Dragging a LoomBlock onto a Canvas creates a Canvas Node referencing the same entity (no copy). Obsidian Canvas idiom.
loom.handshake_native_target: Canvas node referencing LoomBlock id; reuse existing canvas surface (WorkspaceSidebar lists canvases).
loom.notes: Canvas surface exists in app shell; LoomBlock->canvas reference drag not implemented. NO WP-009 MT (MAY-level, Loom Phase 4).

loom.name: Block-as-app views (Kanban/calendar/table/graph over block collections)
loom.description: Multiple interactive presentations (Kanban/calendar/table/graph) over the same LoomBlock collection without duplication. Heaper roadmap H-9; Tana 'every node is typed data' analog.
loom.handshake_native_target: Display projections over LoomBlock queries; future UI framework.
loom.notes: Explicitly Loom Phase 4 (LM-P4-001); NO WP-009 MT. Listed for completeness.

gaps.name: Document-wide find & replace (prose)
gaps.why: A VS Code-class editor MUST have find/replace (regex/case/word/in-selection) across the whole document, not just inside a Monaco code block. No MT owns prose find/replace.
gaps.suggested_target: ProseMirror search/replace plugin over the block-tree + Monaco find for code blocks; add to TiptapMonacoIntegration as a new MT.

gaps.name: Find in files (workspace search UI)
gaps.why: Backend search exists (knowledge_retrieval + loom search) but there is no editor-facing find-in-files panel with results + replace.
gaps.suggested_target: Workspace find panel consuming /knowledge/retrieval + /loom/search; batch replace via document batch ops (MT-157).

gaps.name: Split editors / editor groups + tabs
gaps.why: No side-by-side editing or tabbed open-document management; needed for an editor replacement workbench. Home is §7.1.0 Cross-View Integration but unowned.
gaps.suggested_target: App-shell layout: multiple RichDocumentView panes + tab strip over open KRD ids, all sharing backend authority (no copy).

gaps.name: Diff editor + (CRDT-aware) merge view
gaps.why: No diff/merge surface; revisions exist (versions table) but cannot be visually compared.
gaps.suggested_target: Monaco createDiffEditor for code + block-tree diff for prose, fed by knowledge_rich_document_versions.

gaps.name: Code intelligence bridge (completion/hover/go-to-def/refs/diagnostics/format) wired to code-index
gaps.why: Backend code-nav (symbols/references/file-lens) is BUILT but NOT bridged to Monaco providers; without it the embedded code editor is not VS Code-class.
gaps.suggested_target: Register Monaco Completion/Hover/Definition/Reference/Diagnostic/Formatting providers backed by /knowledge/code/* (offline, project-aware).

gaps.name: Settings / keybindings / themes editor UI
gaps.why: Keymap is code-defined; no user-facing settings/keybindings/theme surface (VS Code settings/keybindings analog).
gaps.suggested_target: Handshake settings surface persisting editor + Monaco prefs + keymap overrides + theme registry.

gaps.name: Prose outline / status bar / breadcrumbs assembled into the editor
gaps.why: Block stable ids + selection_state.ts exist but no outline panel, status bar, or breadcrumb bar is rendered.
gaps.suggested_target: Outline from heading blocks; status bar from selection_state; breadcrumbs from MT-188 API.

gaps.name: Assembled editor workbench shell (toolbar + full command-palette wiring)
gaps.why: The command catalog, keymap, and primitives are BUILT as lib modules, but the integrated editor UI (toolbar bound to EDITOR_COMMANDS, palette wired to the full catalog) is not assembled — so 'full-feature editor' is not yet operator-usable end-to-end.
gaps.suggested_target: Build the RichDocumentView toolbar + command palette against EDITOR_COMMANDS/EDITOR_COMMAND_BY_ID and editor_keymap; this is the single highest-leverage editor assembly task.

gaps.name: Loom four-views workspace surface (All/Unlinked/Sorted/Pins) assembled in UI
gaps.why: All four view queries are BUILT in the backend but there is NO assembled Loom views UI in the app shell (WorkspaceSidebar lists only workspaces/docs/canvases).
gaps.suggested_target: Loom views panel (grid/list toggle, filters, real-time Unlinked shrink) over /loom/views/:view_type — the signature Heaper browsing surface.

gaps.name: Backlinks panel with context snippets + unlinked mentions scanner
gaps.why: get_backlinks + backlink extraction exist, but the reactive backlinks PANEL (with surrounding-text snippets) and the unlinked-MENTIONS scanner (potential-but-unmade links) are missing — the core Heaper/Obsidian navigation payoff.
gaps.suggested_target: Backlinks panel on every block surface (snippet from source_anchor offsets) + title/alias unlinked-mention scan with one-click link.

gaps.name: Folder tree + color labels + sort modes (PostgreSQL-persisted)
gaps.why: No Loom folder tree, colors, labels, or sort modes exist; WorkspaceSidebar is a plain workspace/doc/canvas list.
gaps.suggested_target: PostgreSQL-persisted folder hierarchy/labels/colors/sort (MT-181) + a folder tree panel in the sidebar shell.

gaps.name: Local + global graph views (filters/depth/stale/hub-suppression) rendered
gaps.why: traverse_graph backend is BUILT (local); but filtered local-graph API, global-graph API, and rendered graph views are missing — Obsidian's signature graph idiom.
gaps.suggested_target: MT-179 local-graph (filters/edge-types/depth/stale/citations) + MT-180 global-graph (limits/hub suppression) + a force-graph view component.

gaps.name: Tag hubs (tags as first-class blocks) + nested tags surface
gaps.why: TagHub content type + SubTag edges are modeled, but tags-as-blocks (with their own content, backlinks, nested tags, search filters) are not assembled — a core Heaper differentiator (closest to Tana supertags).
gaps.suggested_target: MT-182: TagHub block surface (content + sub-tags + backlinks) + nested-tag resolution + tag search filters.

gaps.name: Wiki projection compiler + editable overlay (projection-never-authority enforced)
gaps.why: Wiki projection table + WikiLoom renderer exist, but the full compiler (citations/stale-markers/regenerate) and the editable-overlay-without-authority mechanism are missing — and MT-185/MT-187 are where the anti-vault-authority boundary is actively enforced.
gaps.suggested_target: MT-184 compiler (citations + stale markers + regenerate) + MT-185 overlay annotations stored as their own authority rows over a non-authority projection.

gaps.name: LoomBlock <-> ProjectKnowledgeIndex authority bridge (WP-009 core)
gaps.why: The Loom MVP rides on its own loom_blocks/loom_edges tables; WP-009 requires LoomBlock/edge/views/search/backlinks/graph to resolve to ProjectKnowledgeIndex / EventLedger authority (§9.1.1). MT-177 is the foundational bridge that everything else (178-192) depends on.
gaps.suggested_target: MT-177 LoomBlockKnowledgeBridge: link LoomBlocks to knowledge entities/rich-docs/assets/tags/source-spans with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority; reconcile loom_edges with knowledge_edges.

gaps.name: Quick switcher (fuzzy open-anything-by-name)
gaps.why: No fuzzy quick switcher (Obsidian Cmd-O / Roam idiom). MT-186 is operator-search, not the lightweight switcher.
gaps.suggested_target: Title/alias-indexed quick-switch palette over LoomBlocks + rich documents; reuse the command-palette shell.

gaps.name: Cache-tiered media (preview pyramid, proxies, streaming, per-device replication)
gaps.why: Asset proxy/thumbnail fields + thumbnail endpoint exist, but the 4-tier cache (Tier0-3), thumbnail/poster/waveform generation job, video streaming, and per-device replication config are unowned by any WP-009 MT (Loom-roadmap LM-P1-005/LM-P2-006).
gaps.suggested_target: Mechanical preview-generation job (ThumbnailSpec) + tier config persisted per device + on-demand original fetch + video streaming.

gaps.name: Loom visual-debug surface + full FR-EVT-LOOM events + navigation fixtures
gaps.why: Editor has visual_debug.ts; Loom side has partial FR events and no visual-debug snapshots or full navigation fixtures — needed for no-context-model operability and validation.
gaps.suggested_target: MT-190 full FR-EVT-LOOM-001..012 + EventLedger; MT-191 graph/backlink/folder/search debug snapshots + selectors; MT-192 navigation fixtures.

gaps.name: AI Loom jobs (auto-tag/auto-caption/batch-link) confirm-to-promote flow
gaps.why: Derived AI fields + AiSuggested edge type exist, but no AI Loom job pipeline or the suggested->confirmed (DerivedContent->RawContent) promotion flow; no WP-009 MT (Loom Phase 2).
gaps.suggested_target: AI Job Model profiles loom_auto_tag/loom_auto_caption/loom_batch_link writing AiSuggested edges + a user accept/reject loop.

iteration_plan.name: Editor workbench assembly (make the editor operator-usable end-to-end)
iteration_plan.rationale: Primitives, command catalog, keymap, Monaco embed, save/load are BUILT but not assembled. Highest leverage: wire them into a usable VS Code-class editing surface before adding new primitives.
scope: GAP-ED-008 assemble toolbar + full command-palette wiring (EDITOR_COMMANDS/keymap)
GAP-ED-001 prose find/replace + Monaco find for code blocks
ED-WB-002/ED-SEL-001 surface history + status bar from existing version/selection backends
MT-176 Playwright roundtrip suite + MT-175 no-external-app proof
iteration_plan.exit_criteria: Operator can create/edit a rich doc with toolbar+palette+keymap, edit code in Monaco with find, save/load with revisions, and a Playwright suite proves it runs from the Handshake build with no external editor.

iteration_plan.name: LoomBlock<->Knowledge authority bridge (the WP-009 foundation)
iteration_plan.rationale: Everything in LoomObsidianNavigation (178-192) depends on MT-177. Reconcile the Loom MVP (loom_blocks/loom_edges) with ProjectKnowledgeIndex/EventLedger authority per §9.1.1 so Loom is not a parallel store.
scope: GAP-LM-007 MT-177 LoomBlockKnowledgeBridge (link blocks to knowledge entities/rich-docs/assets/tags/spans; Postgres/EventLedger authority)
MT-187 markdown-import-boundary guard (vault never authority) — lock the anti-Obsidian-clone boundary early
iteration_plan.exit_criteria: LoomBlocks/edges resolve to knowledge authority records; importing markdown produces authority rows, never a canonical vault; conformance + fixtures green.

iteration_plan.name: Loom navigation payoff (backlinks + four views + folder tree)
iteration_plan.rationale: Deliver the signature Heaper/Obsidian navigation value on top of the bridge: the four views, backlinks-with-context, unlinked-mentions triage, and the folder tree + colors.
scope: GAP-LM-002 MT-178 backlinks panel (context snippets) + unlinked-mentions scanner
GAP-LM-001 MT-183 four-views UI (All/Unlinked/Sorted/Pins, grid/list, filters, real-time Unlinked shrink)
GAP-LM-003 MT-181 folder tree + color labels + sort modes
GAP-LM-005 MT-182 tag hubs + nested tags + tag search
iteration_plan.exit_criteria: Operator browses the four views, sees reactive backlinks with snippets, triages unlinked mentions, organizes via folder tree/colors, and tags behave as first-class blocks.

iteration_plan.name: Graph, search, wiki projections, retrieval visibility
iteration_plan.rationale: Complete the relational/retrieval surface: local+global graph, cross-source search, wiki compiler + editable overlay (enforcing projection-never-authority), and Loom retrieval-bias visibility.
scope: GAP-LM-004 MT-179 local graph + MT-180 global graph (filters/depth/stale/hub-suppression) + force-graph view
GAP-ED-002 MT-186 cross-source search + find-in-files UI
GAP-LM-006 MT-184 wiki compiler + MT-185 editable overlay (non-authority)
LM-RET-001 MT-189 Loom retrieval-bias visibility over RetrievalTrace
ED-NAV-005/LM-NAV-001 MT-188 breadcrumbs
iteration_plan.exit_criteria: Local+global graph render with filters; cross-source search + find-in-files work; wiki pages generate with citations/stale-markers and accept overlays without becoming authority; retrieval bias reasons are visible.

iteration_plan.name: Code intelligence in Monaco (VS Code-class code editing)
iteration_plan.rationale: Bridge the already-BUILT code-index backend (symbols/references/file-lens) into Monaco providers so the embedded code editor reaches VS Code-class intelligence — the last big editor-replacement gap.
scope: GAP-ED-005 MT-166 Monaco Completion/Hover/Definition/Reference/Diagnostic/Formatting providers over /knowledge/code/*
ED-CODE-INT-006/007/008/009 enable folding/bracket/multi-cursor/semantic-highlight/minimap/sticky/inlay for the embed
GAP-ED-004 Monaco diff editor + block-tree prose diff
GAP-ED-007 prose outline panel
iteration_plan.exit_criteria: In-document code blocks offer completion/hover/go-to-def/refs/diagnostics/format from the project code index, fully offline; diff editor works.

iteration_plan.name: Media cache tiers, AI Loom jobs, diagnostics + fixtures, polish
iteration_plan.rationale: Finish the media pipeline, AI assistance, and the validation/observability surface; address remaining MAY-level and out-of-scope-for-now items.
